title: Microsoft 365 Copilot: From Basics to Fluent

modules.title: What Copilot Actually Does Across the M365 Suite — and What It Doesn't
modules.sub: A clear-eyed baseline on Copilot's real capabilities and limits
modules.description: Learners map where Copilot is embedded across the M365 suite and what it genuinely automates versus what it only partially handles. They assess common misconceptions and identify which limitations will most affect their own work context. This module establishes an honest foundation before any hands-on use begins.

modules.title: Word, Outlook, and Teams: Practical Workflows That Save Real Time
modules.sub: Applying Copilot to writing, email, and meeting workflows
modules.description: Learners work through concrete, repeatable tasks in Word, Outlook, and Teams — including drafting documents, summarizing email threads, and generating meeting recaps. They evaluate which prompts produce useful output and refine their approach when results fall short. By the end, they have at least two time-saving workflows they can apply immediately.

modules.title: Excel and PowerPoint Copilot: Analysis, Visualization, and Presentation
modules.sub: Using Copilot to analyze data and build compelling presentations
modules.description: Learners use Copilot in Excel to interrogate datasets, surface insights, and generate charts without writing formulas from scratch. In PowerPoint, they practice generating slide structures and speaker notes from source material. They also identify where Copilot's outputs require human judgment before being used in professional deliverables.

modules.title: Organizational Data, Privacy Boundaries, and Safe Use Policies
modules.sub: Understanding what data Copilot can access and governance rules
modules.description: Learners examine how Copilot interacts with organizational data through Microsoft Graph and what permission boundaries actually mean in practice. They review common enterprise governance policies and make decisions about what is and isn't appropriate to surface through Copilot in their role. This module builds the judgment needed to use Copilot responsibly within compliance requirements.

modules.title: Advanced Scenarios: Copilot Studio, Agents, and Cross-App Automation
modules.sub: Extending Copilot into custom agents and multi-app workflows
modules.description: Learners explore how Copilot Studio enables organizations to build custom agents tailored to specific business processes. They trace how cross-app automation can connect workflows across M365 and external tools, and assess which scenarios are realistic for their organization today. This module helps learners distinguish between what is available now and what requires additional IT enablement or licensing.

synopsis: Microsoft 365 Copilot is already embedded in the tools most professionals use every day — the question is whether you are using it strategically or barely at all. This course delivers an honest, workflow-first guide to Copilot's real capabilities across Word, Excel, Teams, Outlook, and PowerPoint, including where it underdelivers and how to work around its limits.
rationale: M365 Copilot is one of the fastest-expanding enterprise AI deployments globally, yet most licensed users lack the contextual knowledge to move beyond surface-level prompts into genuine productivity gains.
learning_outcome: Integrate Copilot into at least three recurring professional workflows across M365 applications, with a working understanding of data privacy boundaries and organizational governance requirements.
source_signals: Windows 11 adds AI agent that runs in background with access to personal folders (score: 2617)
nearest_existing: The Centaur and the Tool: Frameworks for Human-AI Teaming
